subject: Sociology
title: The Model Minority Myth
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Model Minority Myth.

hook.text: Asian Americans are called the model minority: hardworking, successful, quiet. The label is a weapon, not a compliment.

buildup.text: The model minority myth portrays Asian Americans as universally successful through hard work and cultural values.

discovery.text: It erases enormous diversity. Hmong, Cambodian, and Laotian Americans have poverty rates above the national average.

twist.text: The myth was promoted in the 1960s to undermine Black civil rights demands: if Asians succeeded, why can't you?

climax.text: It harms Asian Americans by denying their struggles and weaponizes their success against other minorities.

punchline.text: The model minority myth helps no minority. It divides all of them.

quiz.question: Why is the model minority myth harmful?
options: It erases diversity and pits minorities against each other
It accurately describes all Asian Americans
